title: I take decisive, confident action
subtitle: A present-tense affirmation for when the default is to defer rather than decide.
excerpt: A short affirmation from Day 45 of the MONEY program. For when money tasks keep moving down the list because starting feels harder than it should.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 45 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

practice.whenToUse: Use in the morning on a day when you have a specific financial task you've already put off at least once.

content.text: A short affirmation from Day 45 of the MONEY program. The line addresses the specific gap between knowing an action is needed and doing it — the pause that can stretch into days.
I allow decisive, confident action to feel natural and safe for me.
Say it three times. On the last pass, name the specific task you're about to start. Then start it.
Adapted from Day 45 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
